qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unauthenticated feedback CRUD allows data disclosure and manipulation on whitelisted endpoint
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different vulnerable component and root cause: the finding concerns unauthenticated feedback CRUD through /api/v1/feedback whitelist authorization bypass, while the advisory concerns CSVAgent customReadCSV code injection into pyodide.runPythonAsync.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described CSVAgent code injection root cause. The only qualifying finding targets the feedback API authentication whitelist and missing authorization checks, not customReadCSV interpolation or Pyodide execution in CSVAgent.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-9wc7-mj3f-74xv describes Flowise CSVAgent code injection: user-controlled customReadCSV is interpolated into a Python code string and executed by pyodide.runPythonAsync in packages/components/nodes/agents/CSVAgent/CSVAgent.ts.
feedback_idor/vulnerability_report.json describes /api/v1/feedback being included in WHITELIST_URLS and feedback controllers lacking authentication or ownership checks.

The reported code locations for feedback_idor are packages/server/src/utils/constants.ts, packages/server/src/index.ts, feedback routes/controllers, and feedback validation; none are the CSVAgent customReadCSV execution path.
